_notes: Curated list of reference GGUF models for calibr. Purpose: give users a common dataset so benchmark numbers are comparable across different hardware.

Sizes span 135M to 31B so every VRAM budget has something to test. The 18 extra entries (SmolLM2 / TinyLlama / Llama 3.2 / Gemma 2-3 / Phi / Qwen 2.5 / Granite / StableLM / Ministral) added in v0.1.4 give the low + middle presets meaningful variety across model families with different KV-head topologies (Llama / Gemma / Qwen don't share scaling characteristics).
target_dir is relative to scan_paths[0] in config.json. Files land at {scan_paths[0]}/{target_dir}/{hf_file}.
hf_file matches an existing path inside the hf_repo. If a repo is taken down or renamed, edit this file and submit a PR.
Some Gemma-series models require accepting a license on HuggingFace; download may fail with 401 until you do.
size_bytes is informational (used for skip-if-complete); it is not verified against the server.

max_context is the model's officially-supported context length, scraped from HuggingFace config.json. Invoke-Plan uses it to skip Tier A candidates above this ceiling (no point benching ctx=160k on a model that maxes at 128k).

reasoning_mode (Qwen3+ only): 'off' disables the model's thinking / chain-of-thought so prompt_tps and eval_tps reflect direct generation speed instead of internal-deliberation time. 'auto' (default for new Qwen3 entries) leaves it to the model. We use 'off' on Qwen3 entries in this catalog so benchmark numbers are comparable across families.

template_note: free-text reminders about chat-template quirks (notably Gemma's special BOS/EOS handling) that may distort numbers if the wrong template is applied. Surfaced in the report but not yet wired into an automatic flag — TODO for a future version.

models.notes: Smallest Qwen3.5 variant. 8-bit, near-lossless. Good warm-up model.
Unsloth Dynamic 4-bit. Fastest small model in benchmarks.
Multimodal (vision). Pair with mmproj-F16.
Full precision 2B. Quality reference for the model.
Mid-size dense. No mmproj in this release.
The 8GB-VRAM sweet spot. Hybrid SSM+attention, 32 layers, only 8 with KV cache.
Smallest 27B quant. Partial GPU offload required on <= 16GB VRAM.
MoE with ~3B active params. Runs in 8GB VRAM with --n-cpu-moe 34.

Smaller Gemma 4 Effective 2B. Multimodal (vision + audio). Lives in its own folder because its mmproj (n_embd=1536) is incompatible with the E4B sibling's mmproj (n_embd=2560) despite sharing the filename mmproj-F16.gguf.

Effective 4B. Vision + audio encoders; 43 layers fully on GPU in 8GB. Lives in its own folder because its mmproj (n_embd=2560) is incompatible with the E2B sibling's mmproj despite sharing the filename mmproj-F16.gguf.
MoE ~4B active. Comparable to Qwen3.6-35B-A3B routing strategy.
Dense 31B. Requires partial offload on < 20GB VRAM.
Smallest entry in the catalog (~95 MB). Warmup model + sanity check for tiny-model throughput on the box.
Second-smallest. Lets us compare 135M vs 360M scaling on the same architecture.
Pre-reasoning Qwen2.5 baseline at 0.5B. Direct numerical comparison to Qwen3-0.6B with reasoning_mode='off' isolates the family delta.

Qwen3 has a thinking mode that runs an internal chain-of-thought before answering. Forcing 'off' so the bench measures direct token throughput, not internal-deliberation time. With 'on' the eval_tps drops 5-10x because the model 'thinks' before each visible token.
Pre-RoPE Llama architecture, native 2048 ctx. Useful upper-bound on what a tiny dense model can do per second.
Llama 3 family at 1B. Different KV-head topology vs Gemma/Qwen — useful for cross-family scaling comparisons.

models.template_note: Gemma uses BOS/EOS conventions that may distort timing if the wrong template is applied. Investigate before adding more Gemma variants.
models.notes: Gemma 3 family at 1B. Pre-Gemma-4 reference.
Mid-size Qwen2.5 dense. Compare to Qwen3-0.6B reasoning-off to see family/architecture scaling at constant compute.
Stability AI's small reference. 4k native ctx limits Tier A sweep to the first candidate.
Largest SmolLM2. 135M -> 360M -> 1.7B sweep on the same architecture.
IBM Granite 3.0 at 2B. Distinct architecture (GQA + sliding-window attention).

models.template_note: Gemma uses BOS/EOS conventions that may distort timing if the wrong template is applied. Investigate before adding more Gemma variants.
models.notes: Gemma 2 generation at 2B. Bench differences vs Gemma 3 / 4 isolate the architecture iteration delta.
Microsoft Phi-2 at 2.7B. 2k native ctx caps Tier A sweep.
Pre-reasoning Qwen2.5 dense at 3B. Comparable to Qwen3-0.6B per active parameter; useful for the 'is thinking worth the compute?' question.
Llama 3 family at 3B with 128k context. Cross-family scaling vs Qwen2.5-3B (same active params, different KV-head topology).
Mistral's small chat model. Big context (256k) for a 3B class.
Microsoft Phi-3.5 mini at ~3.8B. 128k native ctx; bench against Gemma-3-4B at similar size.

models.template_note: Gemma uses BOS/EOS conventions that may distort timing if the wrong template is applied. Investigate before adding more Gemma variants.
models.notes: Gemma 3 family at 4B. Gemma-3 has 128k native ctx (vs Gemma-2's 8k).
